question:
Will Go Ahead Eagles vs. Feyenoord Rotterdam end in a draw?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 9, 2025
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".

Resolution source: https://eredivisie.nl/competitie/speelschema

evidences.title: Go (game)
evidences.content: compensation points, called komi, which compensate the second player for the first move advantage of their opponent; tournaments commonly use a compensation in the range of 5–8 points, generally including a half-point to prevent draws; handicap stones placed on the board before alternate play, allowing players of different strengths to play competitively (see Go handicap for more information); and superko: Although the basic ko rule described above covers more than 95% of all cycles occurring in games, there are some complex situations—triple ko, eternal life, etc.—that are not covered by it but would allow the game to cycle indefinitely. To prevent this, the ko rule is sometimes extended to forbid the repetition of any previous position. This extension is called superko. evidences.title: Download and install - The Go Programming Language
evidences.content: ``` $ rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.14.3.linux-amd64.tar.gzgo1.14.3.linux-amd64.tar.gz ``` (You may need to run each command separately with the necessary permissions, as root or through `sudo`.) Do not untar the archive into an existing /usr/local/go tree. This is known to produce broken Go installations. 2. Add /usr/local/go/bin to the `PATH` environment variable. You can do this by adding the following line to your $HOME/.profile or /etc/profile (for a system-wide installation): ``` export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin ``` [...] ``` export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin ``` Note: Changes made to a profile file may not apply until the next time you log into your computer. To apply the changes immediately, just run the shell commands directly or execute them from the profile using a command such as `source $HOME/.profile`. 3. Verify that you've installed Go by opening a command prompt and typing the following command: ``` $ go version ``` 4. Confirm that the command prints the installed version of Go. 1. evidences.title: Go (programming language) - Wikipedia
evidences.content: The `interface{}` type can be used to model structured data of any arbitrary schema in Go, such as JSON or YAML data, by representing it as a `map[string] interface{}` (map of string to empty interface). This recursively describes data in the form of a dictionary with string keys and values of any type. Interface values are implemented using pointer to data and a second pointer to run-time type information. Like some other types implemented using pointers in Go, interface values are `nil` if uninitialized. #### Generic code using parameterized types [edit&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Generic code using parameterized types")] Since version 1.18, Go supports generic code using parameterized types. [...] ### Syntax [edit&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Syntax")] Go's syntax includes changes from C "C (programming language)") aimed at keeping code concise and readable. A combined declaration/initialization operator was introduced that allows the programmer to write `i := 3` or `s := "Hello, world!"`, without specifying the types of variables used. This contrasts with C's `int i = 3;` and `char s = "Hello, world!";` (though since C23 "C23 (C standard revision)") type in...

evidences.title: Go Ahead Eagles - Wikipedia
evidences.content: The Go Ahead Eagles are a Dutch professional football club from Deventer in Overijssel. They play in the Eredivisie, and their home stadium since 1920 is De Adelaarshorst. The club won national championships in 1917, 1922, 1930 and 1933, and has produced notable players and managers.
